reportShowcase.title: Report Preview
reportShowcase.subtitle: Here's what the monthly PDF report looks like. Severity, evidence, business impact, and fixes — all in one document.
reportShowcase.findingTitle: API key hardcoded in source code
reportShowcase.findingImpact: Impact: Risk of API abuse and unauthorized charges by third parties
reportShowcase.findingFix: Move to environment variable (process.env.API_KEY) and add .env to .gitignore
reportShowcase.finding2Impact: Impact: Unauthenticated users can access user data
priorities: Move API key to environment variables (Critical — immediate action)
Add auth middleware to admin API (High — PR pending review)
Optimize image assets to improve LCP (Medium)

prExample.title: Auto-fix PR Example
prExample.description: Issues that can be safely fixed are automatically created as reviewed pull requests.

shopify.title: Shopify
shopify.description: Checks Liquid template security, storefront API tokens, app permission scopes, and checkout scripts.

wordpress.title: WordPress
wordpress.description: Checks plugin vulnerabilities, wp-admin access control, REST API auth, file permissions, and wp-config.php protection.

nextjs.title: Next.js
nextjs.description: Checks NEXT_PUBLIC_* env vars, Server Actions auth, API route middleware, and client-side secret leakage.

line.title: LINE Mini App
line.description: Checks X-Line-Signature verification, channel access token management, LIFF URL config, and rate limit handling.

faq.a: For first-time clients, we run a basic security and performance check on one repository for free. The report is delivered via email.
Monthly plans can be canceled anytime. Annual plans require a 1-year commitment but include a 15% discount.

faq.q: Are there limitations on which sites I can audit?
faq.a: We support major platforms including Shopify, WordPress, Next.js, and LINE Mini Apps. GitHub repository access is required.
Reports are delivered as Japanese PDF documents. They include prioritized issue lists, business impact explanations, and specific remediation steps.
For issues that can be safely auto-fixed, we create GitHub pull requests automatically. Just review and merge to apply the fix.
Included in Growth and Pro plans, onboarding covers initial setup support: repository connection, first audit run, and report walkthrough.

aboutPage.title: About Us
aboutPage.subtitle: WebMori is a Tokyo-based web security audit service

sections.title: Mission
sections.description: To make enterprise-level web security accessible to Tokyo freelancers and SMBs. We remove technical complexity so you can focus on your business.

sections.title: Approach
sections.description: Not just vulnerability scanning — we evaluate sites holistically from 5 angles: security, performance, LINE API, i18n, and maintainability. Reports include specific fix proposals with business impact explanations.

sections.title: Technology
sections.description: Our AI-powered audit engine analyzes codebases with the quality of a human security expert, but faster. Auto-fix PRs let you resolve safe issues instantly.

sampleReport.metaDescription: See a sample WebMori audit report. We analyze your site across 5 lenses: security, performance, LINE API, i18n, and maintainability.
sampleReport.title: Sample Audit Report
sampleReport.subtitle: An anonymized sample of an actual audit report. Every plan includes reports like this one.

finding1.title: Missing webhook signature verification
finding1.impact: LINE webhook request signatures are not validated. An attacker could send fake webhook requests, triggering unauthorized data processing.
finding1.fix: Add middleware to verify the X-Line-Signature header using the line-bot-sdk validateSignature function.

finding2.title: Uncompressed image assets (4.2MB total)
finding2.impact: LCP score is 3.8s (target: under 2.5s). Directly impacts mobile user bounce rate, especially on Japanese mobile networks.
finding2.fix: Use next/image component for WebP conversion and proper sizing. Consider using an image CDN.

finding3.title: Inconsistent date formatting
finding3.impact: Some pages show "2026/03/15" while others show "March 15, 2026". Creates an inconsistent experience for Japanese users.
finding3.fix: Standardize on toLocaleDateString('ja-JP') to display dates as "2026年3月15日".

finding4.title: Unused dependencies (3 packages)
finding4.impact: Unnecessarily increases bundle size and build time.

priorities: Implement webhook signature verification — top security priority
Optimize images to improve LCP score — directly impacts conversion rate
Standardize date formatting — improves user trust

tokushoho.title: Specified Commercial Transactions Act
tokushoho.notice: This page is required by the Japanese Specified Commercial Transactions Act (特定商取引法). The original content is in Japanese as required by law.
rows: Disclosed upon request. Please contact support@webmori.jp.
Disclosed upon request. Please contact support@webmori.jp.
support@webmori.jp or via the contact page
Monthly web audit service covering security, performance, LINE API, internationalization, and maintainability. Deliverables include PDF audit reports and pull request fix proposals.
Starter plan: ¥19,800/mo (tax incl.) / Growth plan: ¥39,800/mo (tax incl.) / Pro plan: ¥69,800/mo (tax incl.). Annual plans receive a 15% discount.
Growth plan onboarding: ¥30,000 (tax incl., one-time). Pro plan onboarding: ¥50,000 (tax incl., one-time). No shipping fees. No other additional fees apply.
Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, JCB)
Monthly plans: initial charge at signup, then auto-charged on the same date each month. Annual plans: charged in full at signup, then auto-renewed annually.
Service is available immediately after payment. Initial audit report is delivered within 5 business days of repository registration.
Audit reports (PDF) are delivered via the web dashboard and email. Pull request fix proposals are provided on GitHub.
No fixed period. You may subscribe at any time while the service is listed on our website.
Starter and Growth plans: up to 1 site. Pro plan: up to 2 sites. No other special conditions apply.

Monthly plans can be canceled at any time. Annual plans can be canceled at the end of the contract period. You retain access until the end of your paid period. Cancellation can be processed via the dashboard or by emailing support@webmori.jp.

＜Customer-initiated returns＞ Due to the nature of digital services, returns and exchanges are not accepted after service delivery. Refunds are generally not provided. ＜Defective service＞ If there is a defect in our service, we will promptly re-audit or provide corrections. If the service could not be delivered due to our fault, the applicable period will be refunded. Please contact support@webmori.jp if you notice any issues.
Latest versions of Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Safari, and Microsoft Edge. Internet connection required.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: WebMori is committed to protecting your personal information. This policy explains how we handle personal data when you use our services.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect your email address and name during account registration, repository URLs for audit purposes, session cookies, and information you provide through our contact form.

sections.title: How We Use It
sections.content: We use your information to provide and operate our services, create and deliver audit reports, respond to inquiries, analyze usage for service improvements, and process payments.

sections.title: Third-Party Sharing
sections.content: We do not share personal information with third parties without your consent, except as required by law. We do share necessary data with service providers for payment processing (Stripe) and email delivery (Resend).

sections.title: Data Storage
sections.content: Personal data is stored on servers with appropriate security measures. Data that is no longer needed is promptly deleted.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For questions about personal data handling, please contact support@webmori.jp.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of WebMori's services.

sections.title: Services
sections.content: We provide web security, performance, and related audit services. Audit results are delivered as reports and pull requests.

sections.title: Registration
sections.content: An account is required to use our services. Information provided during registration must be accurate and up to date.

sections.title: Pricing & Payment
sections.content: Service fees are listed on our pricing page. Payment is made by credit card. Monthly plans are auto-renewed each month.

sections.title: Cancellation
sections.content: Monthly plans can be canceled at any time. Annual plans can be canceled at the end of the contract period. You retain access until the end of your paid period.

sections.title: Disclaimer
sections.content: Audit results are informational and do not guarantee detection of all vulnerabilities. Application of suggested fixes is your responsibility.

sections.title: Changes to Terms
sections.content: We may update these terms. Significant changes will be communicated via email.
